memory.summary: My roommate's hygiene is seriously ruining my focus, but since they're friendly and heading out soon, I should just move to the library today instead of starting a fight.
Moving to the library saved my focus today, but it only bought me time; ignoring the hygiene issue means I'll still lose sleep tonight.
I chipped away at the reading response, but ignoring my roommate's hygiene issue just delays the inevitable sleep loss I promised myself I'd avoid.

I made progress on the reading response, but that extra hour of isolation just pushed my sleep debt further away. I'm getting work done while ignoring the hygiene fight, and it feels like a losing battle for my rest.

I'm making progress on the reading response, but I keep choosing to ignore my roommate's hygiene issue just to stay in the library. This short-term focus is only delaying the sleep loss I promised myself I'd avoid.
Roommate_R isn't being mean, just stuck in a slump, so I can address the smell directly without hurting our bond. Time to stop delaying and make a kind request now.
My direct talk with roommate_R landed well, preserving our bond while I finally stop delaying the hygiene fix. This connection matters more than my extra hour of isolation.
I fixed the hygiene issue and kept my bond intact, which feels better than grinding on that reading response. Now I'm done delaying; time to eat with my friends instead of hiding in the library.
The direct talk worked perfectly, proving our bond is stronger than any deadline. Now I'll drop the reading response and grab dinner with my friends instead of hiding in the library.
My direct confrontation worked, but the lingering smell suggests I need to follow up on the hygiene fix before we all get sick. I'm still too tired to finish that reading response tonight anyway.
That tiny 0.02 progress on the reading response feels pointless compared to the real win of keeping my bond intact and finally ditching the library.
That measly 0.02 progress confirms I was right to ditch the library; my bond with the group matters infinitely more than this tiny academic gain.

desires: To immediately crash and recharge without feeling guilty about wasting 'productive' time on the reading response.
To validate my decision to ditch the library by physically being present with my friends tonight, proving that our bond outweighs this soft deadline.
To feel a rush of relief from finally confronting the hygiene issue directly, rather than stewing in passive avoidance.
To ensure I don't look like I'm flaking on the group dinner plans now that I've already made up my mind to prioritize them.

goals.description: Hit 'done' on the reading response draft immediately to close the loop, then ditch the library and head straight to Cohort_3 for dinner so I can be present with my people instead of carrying this guilt.
goals.rationale: I just hit 73% progress; that's enough runway. The soft deadline is Wednesday, but my affiliation drive is screaming right now that if I stay here one more minute looking at text while my friends are waiting, I'm going to feel like a dead weight. I need to physically leave this space to validate that our bond matters more than this assignment. If I drag this out hoping to get it perfect, I'll just end up resenting the whole group later. Done now, eat with them, clear my conscience.

agent.last_reasoning: I've hit my runway; staying here one more minute feels like dead weight. I need to text Cohort_3 now so they don't feel ignored. Defer the reading response until tonight after dinner to prioritize being present with my people.

I'll do it right after lunch if the library work goes well.
